title: centaurea variegata subf. matthiolifolia
intro: Centaurea variegata subf. matthiolifolia, also known as the blue cornflower, is a plant species in the daisy family. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Centaurea variegata subf. matthiolifolia include its ability to reduce fever, soothe skin irritations, and act as an antispasmodic. Herbal preparations of this plant often include teas, infusions, and topical applications to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
